title: Settings (SystemX)
description: Configuration for the SystemX JupyterLab extension.

greedyNotebookExtraction.title: Exhaustive Context Capture
greedyNotebookExtraction.description: When enabled, the engine treats the notebook as a sequential script (top-to-bottom).

rawDatasetPath.title: Labeling Dataset
rawDatasetPath.description: The workspace-relative dataset directory used by the 'Next' notebook iterator. Datasets under ./data (any folder containing a notebooks/ subdirectory) are auto-discovered and offered as a dropdown; you can still type a custom path.

defaultFetchMode.title: Default Labeling Mode
defaultFetchMode.description: The default mode when fetching the next notebook (unannotated, annotated, or flagged).

defaultFlagType.title: Default Target Flag
defaultFlagType.description: If the default mode is 'flagged', which flag type should the engine target by default?

replaceDataflowInUDFs.title: UDF Scope Inlining
replaceDataflowInUDFs.description: Attempts to inline internal dataflow of UDFs.

detectDsl.title: DSL Operator Interception
detectDsl.description: Rewrites operator-overloading DSLs (Apache Beam pipelines, Airflow task dependencies, pipe libraries) into dataflow before graph construction.

highlightRelevantSubgraphs.title: Deterministic Lineage Pruning
highlightRelevantSubgraphs.description: Aggressively removes topological paths that do not contribute to an Export.

useGraphRefiner.title: Post-Inference Heuristic Refiner
useGraphRefiner.description: Applies a deterministic rule-engine after neural classification.

usePredictionForAnnotation.title: Pre-labeled Annotations
usePredictionForAnnotation.description: Use SystemX model for initial prediction when using the Annotations module.

explainFeatureImportance.title: Feature Importance Explanations
explainFeatureImportance.description: Compute per-node feature importance during labeling and show it in the node sidebar. Slower; supported by the HGT and XGBoost backends.

modelVariant.title: Model Variant
modelVariant.description: Optional specific model checkpoint (manifest key) to use for the Lineage and Annotate views — chiefly a fine-tuned variant created by the Retrain button (e.g. xgboost_standard_ft20260709_153000). When set, it overrides the Model + Feature Set selection above. Leave blank to use the base model chosen above. The SystemX settings panel offers a dropdown of available variants.

nnBackend.title: Lineage & Labeling Model
nnBackend.description: Model family used for COMPUTE_HUB classification in the Lineage and Annotate views. HGT / MLP / XGBoost each require a checkpoint discovered from manifest.json in v2_checkpoints_dir (see jupyter_server_config.py). The learned models are combined with the Feature Set below.
enumDescriptions: Multi-layer perceptron over node features (no graph attention)
XGBoost gradient-boosted trees over node features
Vamsa KB static lookup (no neural model; ignores Feature Set)

featurePreset.title: Feature Set
featurePreset.description: Feature preset the selected model was trained with. Selects which checkpoint (manifest key <model>_<preset>) is used. NOTE: this setting has no effect when the Model is 'vamsa_static' (it is a static KB lookup with no learned features).
enumDescriptions: Code + API + library embeddings and all structural features (default)

execOrderBackend.title: Execution Order Predictor
execOrderBackend.description: Predictor used by the Execution State view. The heuristic needs no checkpoint; the learned predictors refine ambiguous bindings and tie-breaking and require an exec_order_* checkpoint in manifest.json (they fall back to the heuristic when not loaded).
enumDescriptions: Dependency-graph topological sort with document-order tie-breaking (no model needed)
Pairwise cell-precedence MLP refining ambiguity resolution and tie-breaking
Pairwise cell-precedence XGBoost refining ambiguity resolution and tie-breaking

debounceDelay.title: ML Analysis Throttle (ms)
debounceDelay.description: Applies ONLY to the ML pipelines (Lineage, Annotate, Execution State). After you stop typing in a cell, the extension waits this long before running them, and runs at most one at a time (a new run starts only once you've stopped typing and nothing is already running — edits are never queued). Non-ML views (AST, Dataflow, Vamsa, Environment) always recompute immediately with no throttle. Use -1 to disable ML auto-recompute (they then update only when you run a cell).

applyGlobalTheme.title: Apply SystemX Global Theme
applyGlobalTheme.description: Restyle the whole JupyterLab UI — code cells, sidebar, toolbars, status bar and colors — to match the SystemX design. Turn off to keep JupyterLab's default appearance (the SystemX panels themselves are unaffected). Takes effect immediately.

enabled.title: Record Execution Order
enabled.description: Observe every cell execution and keep an ordered, kernel-session-segmented trace per notebook. Purely observational — nothing is written into the notebook file.

autosave.title: Trace Autosave
enumDescriptions: Never save automatically (manual Save button only).
Save shortly after each burst of executions (see delay below).
Save when a kernel session ends (restart, kernel change, or closing the notebook).
autosave.description: When to persist the recorded trace as a sidecar JSON on the server.

autosaveDelay.title: Autosave Delay (ms)
autosaveDelay.description: Trailing debounce for the 'debounced' autosave mode: the trace is saved this long after the last recorded execution.

storagePath.title: Trace Storage Path
storagePath.description: Workspace-relative directory for trace sidecars (saved under '<path>/traces/'). Recorded traces double as gold ordering supervision for the learned execution-order predictors.
